title: The Economic Challenges Facing the UK: Brexit and Public Debt
subtitle: New data reveals the long-term impact of leaving the EU as the government struggles with rising borrowing costs.

summary.en: Recent analysis suggests that Brexit has reduced the size of the UK economy by 6% over the last decade. This decline is linked to both initial uncertainty and new trade barriers. Meanwhile, the government is facing pressure as national borrowing costs reach record highs. Official figures show that spending on debt interest has increased significantly. Leaders are now debating how to manage these fragile public finances while balancing the needs of the country.
summary.zh: 近期分析顯示，脫歐導致英國過去十年的經濟規模縮減了百分之六。此衰退與最初的不確定性及新的貿易壁壘有關。同時，隨著國家借貸成本達到歷史新高，政府正面臨壓力。官方數據顯示，債務利息支出顯著增加。領袖們目前正針對如何在平衡國家需求的同時，管理這些脆弱的公共財政進行辯論。

paragraphs.en: As the United Kingdom approaches the ten-year anniversary of the Brexit referendum, experts are taking a closer look at the long-term financial consequences of leaving the European Union. According to new analysis from the Bank of England, the UK economy is 6% smaller than it would have been if the country had remained in the EU. This study used internal company data to track how businesses reacted to the vote and the subsequent changes in trade rules.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著英國即將迎來脫歐公投十週年，專家們正深入審視脫離歐盟所帶來的長期財務後果。根據英格蘭銀行（Bank of England）的最新分析，若英國當初選擇留在歐盟，其經濟規模將比現在大上百分之六。這項研究利用企業內部數據，追蹤了各界對於公投結果以及隨後貿易規則變動的反應。

paragraphs.en: Researchers found that the economic hit came from two main sources. First, there was the initial surprise and uncertainty that followed the 2016 vote. Second, the UK faced new trade barriers after leaving the EU’s customs union and single market in 2021. Professor Nick Bloom, a co-author of the study from Stanford University, noted that the UK was growing quickly before the referendum. He argued that the country could have kept pace with other major economies if not for the disruption caused by Brexit.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人員發現，經濟衝擊主要源於兩個面向。首先是二〇一六年公投後隨之而來的震驚與不確定性；其次是英國在二〇二一年退出歐盟關稅同盟與單一市場後，面臨了新的貿易壁壘。史丹佛大學（Stanford University）教授、該研究共同作者尼克·布魯姆（Nick Bloom）指出，英國在公投前經濟成長迅速。他認為，若非脫歐造成的混亂，英國本可與其他主要經濟體保持同步成長。

paragraphs.en: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey recently spoke about these consequences, explaining that the reduction in export markets has naturally led to lower economic activity. He noted that while the impact on the financial services sector was negative, it was not as severe as some early predictions had suggested. However, some economists warn that it is difficult to measure exactly how much growth was lost, especially when global events like the energy crisis have also affected the world economy.
paragraphs.zh: 英格蘭銀行總裁安德魯·貝利（Andrew Bailey）近期談及這些後果時解釋，出口市場的萎縮自然導致經濟活動減少。他提到，雖然脫歐對金融服務業的影響負面，但並未如部分早期預測那般嚴重。然而，一些經濟學家警告，要精確衡量損失的成長幅度相當困難，特別是在能源危機等全球事件同樣影響世界經濟的情況下。

paragraphs.en: While the long-term effects of Brexit remain a topic of debate, the government is also dealing with immediate financial pressures. Official figures show that the UK borrowed £23.3bn in May, which is nearly one-third higher than the same month last year. This figure was £5.6bn higher than what the Office for Budget Responsibility (OBR) — an independent group that monitors government spending — had predicted.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管脫歐的長期影響仍是爭論焦點，政府目前也正處理迫在眉睫的財務壓力。官方數據顯示，英國五月份借貸金額達二百三十三億英鎊，比去年同期高出近三分之一。此數字比預算責任辦公室（Office for Budget Responsibility, OBR）——一個監督政府支出的獨立機構——所預測的金額高出了五十六億英鎊。

paragraphs.en: Much of this increase is due to the rising cost of interest on government debt. The Office for National Statistics (ONS) reported that interest payments reached £11.7bn in May, the highest amount ever recorded for that month. Danni Hewson, an analyst at AJ Bell, explained that higher inflation has made borrowing more expensive. Inflation refers to a general increase in prices and a fall in the purchasing value of money. When inflation is high, the cost of borrowing money for the government typically rises as well.
paragraphs.zh: 這筆增額大多歸因於政府債務利息成本的攀升。國家統計局（Office for National Statistics, ONS）報告指出，五月份利息支出達到一百一十七億英鎊，創下該月份的歷史新高。AJ Bell 的分析師丹妮·休森（Danni Hewson）解釋，高通膨使得借貸成本變得更加昂貴。通膨是指物價普遍上漲及貨幣購買力下降的現象。當通膨高漲時，政府的借貸成本通常也會隨之上升。

paragraphs.en: These financial difficulties have created a challenging environment for political leaders. Ruth Gregory, an economist at Capital Economics, described the public finances as “fragile,” noting that this situation will limit the choices available to any Prime Minister. The government is currently trying to balance the need to protect families from rising costs with the goal of reducing national debt. Chief Secretary to the Treasury Lucy Rigby stated that the government has a plan to deal with these challenges, emphasizing that they are working to cut borrowing faster than other major economies.
paragraphs.zh: 這些財務困境為政治領袖創造了艱難的環境。凱投宏觀（Capital Economics）的經濟學家露絲·格雷戈里（Ruth Gregory）形容公共財政「脆弱」，並指出這種情況將限制任何首相可選擇的政策空間。政府目前正試圖在保護家庭免受物價上漲衝擊，與削減國家債務的目標之間取得平衡。財政部首席秘書露西·里格比（Lucy Rigby）表示，政府已有應對這些挑戰的計畫，並強調他們正致力於比其他主要經濟體更快地削減借貸。

paragraphs.en: At the same time, political competition is intensifying. There is talk of a potential leadership challenge within the Labour Party, with figures like Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham being mentioned. Observers note that any candidate for leadership will need to show they can manage the economy carefully. Mr. Burnham has already pledged to follow existing fiscal rules, which include not borrowing money to pay for daily government expenses. This approach is seen by some as a way to signal stability to investors.
paragraphs.zh: 與此同時，政治競爭正趨於激烈。工黨內部傳出潛在的領導權挑戰，包括大曼徹斯特市長安迪·伯納姆（Andy Burnham）等人物皆被提及。觀察家指出，任何領導人候選人皆需展現其審慎管理經濟的能力。伯納姆先生已承諾遵守現有的財政規則，其中包括不透過借貸來支付日常政府開支。此舉被部分人士視為向投資人傳達穩定訊號的方式。

paragraphs.en: Despite these economic hurdles, there are some small signs of activity in other areas. Separate figures show that retail spending rose by 1.2% in May. Retailers reported that good weather helped increase sales of items like outdoor furniture and fans. While this is a positive sign for the high street, it does not solve the larger, more complex issues facing the national budget.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管面臨這些經濟障礙，其他領域仍出現了一些微小的活躍跡象。個別數據顯示，五月份零售支出成長了百分之一點二。零售商回報稱，良好的天氣有助於戶外家具與電風扇等商品的銷售。雖然這對商業街來說是個正面訊號，但並未解決國家預算所面臨更龐大且複雜的問題。

paragraphs.en: As the UK moves forward, the government must navigate the combined effects of the post-Brexit transition and the current global economic climate. With borrowing costs high and public services requiring funding, the path ahead remains difficult. Whether the economy can recover its lost growth or if the current financial strain will continue to limit public spending remains a key question for the years to come.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著英國持續前行，政府必須應對脫歐後過渡期與當前全球經濟局勢的雙重影響。在借貸成本高昂且公共服務亟需資金的情況下，前方的道路依然艱難。經濟能否恢復失去的成長，或是當前的財務壓力將持續限制公共支出，仍是未來幾年關鍵的疑問。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To move or develop at the same speed as someone or something else.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個常見的慣用語，用來形容發展速度與他人一致。文中：He argued that the country could have kept pace with other major economies if not for the disruption caused by Brexit.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The main street in a town where most of the shops and businesses are located.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 大街；商業街（指市中心主要的購物區）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個英國英語中非常常見的詞彙，但在台灣英語學習者中較少見，容易被誤解為單純的「高處的街道」。文中：While this is a positive sign for the high street, it does not solve the larger, more complex issues facing the national budget.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To show or make known a feeling, opinion, or intention.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（信號），這裡作動詞用，表示透過行為來向外界傳遞某種訊息。文中：This approach is seen by some as a way to signal stability to investors.

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: Brexit cost 6% of UK economy, Bank of England company data suggests

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: Warning over 'fragile' public finances as borrowing rises

quiz.question: According to the article, what was the primary reason for the record-high interest payments recorded in May?

options.A: The government's decision to increase spending on public services.
options.B: The impact of higher inflation on the cost of borrowing.
options.C: The unexpected costs associated with the Brexit transition.
options.D: A significant rise in retail spending across the country.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第五段，分析師 Danni Hewson 明確指出「higher inflation has made borrowing more expensive」，且隨後提到當通貨膨脹高時，政府借貸成本通常會上升。選項 A 並非文中提到的主因；選項 C 雖然文章有提到 Brexit 的影響，但與五月份利息支出激增的直接原因（通膨）不同；選項 D 的零售支出增加是另一回事，與政府債務利息無關。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the current state of the UK's political landscape regarding economic management?

options.A: Political leaders are prioritizing tax cuts to stimulate the economy.
options.B: The government is expected to abandon fiscal rules to pay for daily expenses.
options.C: Future political leaders will likely need to demonstrate fiscal caution to gain trust.
options.D: The Labour Party has already decided to replace its current leadership.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第七段，文中提到潛在的領導人選（如 Andy Burnham）承諾遵守財政規則，且此舉被視為向投資者傳遞「穩定性」的訊號。這暗示了在目前脆弱的財務環境下，展現謹慎的經濟管理能力是政治人物贏得信任的必要條件。選項 A 文中未提及；選項 B 與文中提到的「遵守現有財政規則」相反；選項 D 僅提到有「談論」潛在挑戰，並未確認已決定更換領導層。

quiz.question: In the sixth paragraph, what does the word 'fragile' mean in the context of the description of public finances?

options.C: Growing at a very slow pace.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在第六段中，經濟學家 Ruth Gregory 形容公共財政為「fragile」，意指其處於脆弱、不穩定的狀態，容易受到外部衝擊（如高通膨或債務壓力）的影響而崩潰。選項 B 雖然財政問題複雜，但 fragile 強調的是脆弱性而非複雜度；選項 C 與成長速度無直接關聯；選項 D 雖然文中提到投資者，但 fragile 本身並非指依賴性，而是指狀態的脆弱。

quiz.question: What is the central message of this article regarding the UK's current economic situation?

options.A: The UK economy is recovering quickly due to a rise in retail spending.
options.B: Brexit is the sole cause of all current financial difficulties in the UK.
options.C: The government has successfully eliminated national debt through strict fiscal rules.
options.D: The UK faces a difficult economic future caused by both Brexit and global financial pressures.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章開頭探討 Brexit 的長期影響，中間分析了通膨與借貸成本的壓力，最後一段總結提到政府必須同時應對「後 Brexit 轉型」與「全球經濟氣候」的雙重影響。選項 A 過度樂觀，文中提到零售增加並不能解決大問題；選項 B 過於絕對，文章也提到了全球能源危機的影響；選項 C 與文中提到的政府仍在努力減少債務且面臨壓力不符。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
